guidanceSemantics.nonCertificationStatement: DSFB findings may support internal review against standards-oriented expectations, but DSFB does not certify compliance with IEC, ISO, RTCA, MIL, NIST, or other standards.

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from safety surface, correctness-critical Power-of-Ten rules, and correctness-oriented structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Correctness

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from lifecycle/governance evidence, reviewability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules, and maintainability-heavy structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Maintainability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from async/concurrency structural checks and bounded-control-flow review signals.
advisorySubscores.title: Concurrency / Async

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from runtime-allocation proxies, resource-lifecycle checks, and bounded-allocation / bounded-loop review rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Resource Discipline

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from verification signals, build/tooling complexity, and analyzability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Verification / Reviewability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from the full locked rubric as a broad readiness-oriented advisory synthesis.
advisorySubscores.title: Assurance / Provenance

guideline: Method: weighted checkpoint scoring across Safety (15%), Verification (15%), Build/Tooling (10%), Lifecycle/Governance (10%), NASA/JPL Power of Ten (25%), and Advanced Structural Checks (25%).
Checkpoint credit: pass/clear/applied = 1.0, indeterminate/partial = 0.5, elevated/not applied = 0.0.
Fairness rule: raw motif counts do not linearly reduce the score; each checkpoint contributes once so large crates are not punished simply for having more code.
Informational-only signals such as DSFB heuristic motif matches, hotspot counts, and capability flags like no_std/no_alloc are reported but excluded from the score denominator.
Interpretation: this is a broad improvement and review-readiness score for source-visible controls and evidence, not a certification and not a measure of runtime correctness.

conclusionLenses.certificationPreparation: For certification-oriented preparation, treat ITER-UNB, JPL-R9, NASA-CC, SAFE-STATE, TIME-WAIT as pre-review cleanup targets and evidence-organizing prompts rather than certification outcomes.
conclusionLenses.complianceReadiness: 7 finding(s) directly affect analyzability, reproducibility, or review traceability. DSFB may support internal review against standards-oriented expectations, but it does not certify compliance.
conclusionLenses.distributedOperational: Operational pressure is most visible in ITER-UNB, P10-5, H-ALLOC-01, H-SERDE-01, H-THRU-01. These findings are the most likely to matter later in runtime replay, backpressure review, or production-style load investigation.
conclusionLenses.rustMaintainer: Use the 58.9% overall score as a broad code-improvement target, not a compliance or certification badge. The highest-value maintainer work is concentrated in JPL-R9, NASA-CC, SAFE-STATE, TIME-WAIT, JPL-R0.

findings.remediation: Add `.take(...)`, explicit bounds, or documented finite-source guarantees on terminal iterator consumption.
findings.title: Unbounded iterator terminal-consumption audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add a bound, trusted finite-source proof, or regression test that demonstrates the iterator cannot grow without limit.

findings.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
findings.title: Unchecked extraction / dereference safety audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

findings.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
findings.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot audit (NASA SWE-220 proxy)
findings.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

findings.remediation: Make fallback states explicit and document what the safe-state behavior is for the affected control path.
findings.title: Catch-all state handling / safe-state fallback audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add tests that drive the fallback path explicitly and confirm the intended safe-state behavior is named, not implied.

findings.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
findings.title: Hard-coded timing assumption audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

findings.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
findings.title: Recursion and cyclic call graph audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

findings.remediation: Constrain dynamic loading behind verification, sandboxing, or explicit operator review.
findings.title: Dynamic loading / plugin sandbox audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add review notes or CI checks that prove the dynamic-loading boundary is verified, sandboxed, or intentionally excluded from trusted paths.

findings.remediation: Use `write_all`, retry `Interrupted`, or document why partial writes are already handled by the caller.
findings.title: Partial-write / Interrupted handling audit
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add IO-path tests that inject Interrupted or partial writes and prove the caller handles them correctly.

findings.remediation: Move dynamic allocation to initialization paths or document and bound the steady-state allocation sites.
findings.title: No dynamic allocation after initialization
findings.verificationSuggestion: Profile the flagged path under steady-state load and confirm no avoidable heap growth remains after initialization.

findings.remediation: Split large functions into reviewable units with clearer local invariants and narrower responsibilities.
findings.title: Functions stay within a single-sheet size budget (~60 LOC)
findings.verificationSuggestion: Split the function and add narrower tests that name the local invariants introduced by the refactor.

findings.remediation: Replace catch-all control flow with explicit state handling or document the fallback state as intentional.
findings.title: Assertion density averages at least two per function
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add state-transition tests that cover the previously catch-all path explicitly.

findings.remediation: Propagate errors explicitly rather than unwrapping, or document the invariant that justifies the unwrap/expect.
findings.title: Return values are checked and parameters are validated
findings.verificationSuggestion: Replace unwrap/expect with explicit handling or add an invariant test that proves the extraction precondition.

findings.remediation: Reduce conditional-compilation forks or document why each feature/macro path remains auditable.
findings.title: Conditional compilation and metaprogramming stay minimal
findings.verificationSuggestion: Review feature/macro-expanded paths and add CI coverage for the meaningful forks.

findings.remediation: Remove recursion where possible, or isolate the pattern behind a bounded proof and explicit review note.
findings.title: Simple control flow; no recursion or equivalent escapes
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add a focused test or review note that proves the remaining recursion is bounded, or refactor it into an explicit loop/work queue.

findings.remediation: Add explicit upper bounds, timeout guards, or fixed-step limits so loop behavior is reviewable.
findings.title: All loops have a fixed upper bound
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that demonstrates a visible loop bound, timeout, or cancellation path on the flagged logic.

findings.remediation: Audit hot-loop allocation sites and prefer bounded or reserved growth on steady-state paths.
findings.title: Monotonic increase in allocation latency with step-change at capacity doubling
findings.verificationSuggestion: Benchmark the flagged path under steady load and inspect allocation counts before and after preallocation changes.

findings.remediation: Review payload growth, eager allocation, and schema-boundary handling on the serialization path.
findings.title: Serialization latency increasing with step-change at schema boundary
findings.verificationSuggestion: Review the emitted evidence and add a targeted regression or replay check on the affected path.

findings.remediation: Keep warnings and analyzer gates active in CI so the audit surface stays reviewable over time.
findings.title: Pedantic warnings and static analyzers are enforced
findings.verificationSuggestion: Keep analyzer and warnings-as-errors gates in CI and record the expected toolchain surface in the repo docs.

findings.remediation: Prefer monotonic clocks for control logic and isolate wall-clock use to presentation or external protocol boundaries.
findings.title: Monotonic drift in timestamp-derived residuals between nodes
findings.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that isolates monotonic timing logic from wall-clock presentation or protocol boundaries.

findings.remediation: Inspect hot paths for hidden copies, queue growth, or retry behavior that can erode throughput before alarms fire.
findings.title: Persistent throughput decline not attributable to workload reduction
findings.verificationSuggestion: Review the emitted evidence and add a targeted regression or replay check on the affected path.

findings.remediation: Review partial-write handling, retry damping, timeout paths, and whether network assumptions are made explicit.
findings.title: Burst of retransmits followed by drift in RTT variance
findings.verificationSuggestion: Review the emitted evidence and add a targeted regression or replay check on the affected path.
